entity.summary: Australia-based cryptocurrency exchange established in 2013, registered with AUSTRAC as a virtual-asset service provider and reporting an Australian Financial Services Licence for non-cash payment facilities from April 2026.
entity.notes: Official and independent material support the 2013 establishment year. CoinSpot states that it has been registered with AUSTRAC since May 8, 2018 and, as of April 29, 2026, holds an AFSL with non-cash-payments authorisation. These regulatory markers do not imply government endorsement of crypto trading or assets.

expected.summary: Australia-based centralized exchange launched in 2013 and still operating under the CoinSpot brand.

changes.summary: Australia-based cryptocurrency exchange established in 2013, registered with AUSTRAC as a virtual-asset service provider and reporting an Australian Financial Services Licence for non-cash payment facilities from April 2026.
changes.notes: Official and independent material support the 2013 establishment year. CoinSpot states that it has been registered with AUSTRAC since May 8, 2018 and, as of April 29, 2026, holds an AFSL with non-cash-payments authorisation. These regulatory markers do not imply government endorsement of crypto trading or assets.

events.title: CoinSpot was established in Australia in 2013
events.description: CoinSpot was established in Australia in 2013 as a cryptocurrency exchange and brokerage platform.
events.notes: The reviewed sources establish the year but not an exact launch day, so January 1 is used as a year-level marker.

events.title: CoinSpot registered with AUSTRAC
events.description: CoinSpot states that it became registered with AUSTRAC as a virtual-asset service provider on May 8, 2018 under Australia's anti-money-laundering and counter-terrorism-financing framework.
events.notes: AUSTRAC registration imposes AML and reporting obligations; it is not an endorsement of the platform or its listed assets.

events.title: CoinSpot reported an Australian Financial Services Licence
events.description: CoinSpot reported that it began holding an Australian Financial Services Licence with a non-cash-payments authorisation on April 29, 2026.
events.notes: The reviewed source is CoinSpot's current regulatory disclosure. HEI records only the stated non-cash-payments scope and does not infer general authorisation of all crypto services.

evidence.title: Trade Safely With CoinSpot
evidence.notes: Official CoinSpot page states that the platform was established in 2013.

evidence.title: CoinSpot company profile
evidence.notes: Independent industry profile corroborates the 2013 founding and Australian exchange identity.

evidence.title: Is CoinSpot Regulated?
evidence.notes: Current official disclosure states that CoinSpot has been registered with AUSTRAC as a virtual-asset service provider since May 8, 2018.

evidence.title: New Australian laws to regulate cryptocurrency providers
evidence.notes: AUSTRAC's primary notice documents the 2018 mandatory registration and AML/CTF framework for Australian digital-currency exchanges.

evidence.title: Is CoinSpot Regulated?
evidence.notes: CoinSpot's current disclosure states that, as of April 29, 2026, it holds an AFSL with a non-cash-payments authorisation.

evidence.title: CoinSpot official exchange
evidence.notes: The official domain verifies the current CoinSpot identity and active exchange platform.
